_meta.description: NRC IRAP (Industrial Research Assistance Program) — federal R&D funding for innovative small businesses. Most relevant for tech, software, and product development businesses.

program_name_en: NRC IRAP — Industrial Research Assistance Program
program_name_fr: PARI-CNRC — Programme d'aide à la recherche industrielle
plain_language_summary: IRAP provides funding and advisory services for Canadian small businesses doing technological innovation or R&D. If you're building a software product, developing a new technology, or creating an innovative process — not just providing a service — IRAP may cover up to 80% of eligible project costs. Very underused by small tech businesses.

amount.advisory_services: Free — Industrial Technology Advisors (ITAs) provide free technical and business advice
amount.financial_contributions: Project-based — typically $50,000 to $500,000 for SMEs; micro-projects can be smaller
amount.youth_employment: Separate stream: up to $25,000 to hire a post-secondary student for an R&D project
amount.note: IRAP doesn't have a fixed maximum — it depends on your project scope and eligible costs

strong_fit: Tech consulting with proprietary tool development
Any business developing a new product or process
weak_fit: Service businesses with no R&D component
Standard food preparation (unless innovating a process or product)
business_type.key_question: Are you developing something technically new, or just providing an existing service?

company_size.note: IRAP is specifically for small and medium enterprises — no minimum size

immigration_status.note: Company must be incorporated in Canada or be a Canadian sole proprietor

eligible: Early stage with a defined R&D project
Growth stage with new product/process development
business_stage.not_eligible: Pure service delivery with no innovation component

how_it_works.step_1: Contact an NRC Industrial Technology Advisor (ITA) — they're assigned by region and provide free advisory services first
how_it_works.step_2: ITA helps you identify if you have an eligible project and guides your application
how_it_works.step_3: Apply with a defined project: technical objectives, timeline, budget, expected outcomes
how_it_works.step_4: If approved, funding covers a portion of eligible costs (salaries, contractor fees, some materials)
how_it_works.note: The ITA relationship is valuable beyond funding — they provide free technical mentorship

sred_comparison.sred_note: SR&ED (Scientific Research & Experimental Development) is a related federal tax credit that many small tech businesses also qualify for. IRAP and SR&ED can sometimes be combined — ask your ITA about this.

persona_fit.Marcus_freelance_dev: Potentially strong fit if Marcus is building proprietary tools or platforms — not just hourly consulting. If he's developing a software product, IRAP + SR&ED could be very valuable.
persona_fit.Yara_bakery: Weak fit unless she's developing a novel food product or automated process

timeline: ITA assignment: 1-2 weeks. Application to decision: 6-12 weeks.
common_mistakes: Thinking IRAP is only for large tech companies — it actively targets small businesses
Not contacting an ITA first — the ITA guides you through eligibility, don't apply cold
Describing your work as 'services' when it has genuine R&D elements — reframe around the innovation
Missing SR&ED which is complementary — always ask your ITA and accountant about combining both
